title: The History of Newspapers
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: The newspaper has (0) an essential role in society for centuries, keeping citizens informed about current events and holding those in power to account. The first regular newspapers appeared in Europe in the early seventeenth century, though printed news sheets had been in (1) even earlier. The invention of the printing press made it possible to produce multiple copies quickly, (2) newspapers accessible to a wider audience. Throughout the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, newspapers grew in influence, often (3) public opinion on political matters. The rise of investigative journalism in the twentieth century led to some remarkable achievements, including exposing government corruption and (4) about significant social reforms. However, the newspaper industry has faced serious (5) in recent decades. The internet has transformed how people consume news, and many traditional publications have struggled to (6) to this new landscape. Advertising revenue, once the financial backbone of newspapers, has shifted to online platforms. Despite these difficulties, quality journalism remains as important as ever. In an age of fake news and misinformation, the need for reliable, well-researched reporting has never been (7). Many newspapers have successfully transitioned to digital formats, finding new ways to (8) with readers and generate income through subscriptions.

example.explanation: 'Played a role' es una colocación fija para expresar que algo tiene una función o importancia.

questions.explanation: 'In circulation' es la expresión correcta para indicar que algo está siendo distribuido o usado.
'Making something accessible' es la construcción correcta para indicar que algo se vuelve accesible.
'Shaping public opinion' es la colocación correcta para describir la influencia sobre la opinión pública.
'Bringing about reforms' es el phrasal verb correcto que significa provocar o causar cambios.
'Faced challenges' es la colocación más apropiada en contextos formales para describir obstáculos.
'Adapt to' es el verbo correcto para describir el proceso de cambiar para enfrentar nuevas circunstancias.
'Greater' es el adjetivo correcto para intensificar conceptos abstractos como 'need'.
'Engage with readers' es la colocación correcta para describir la interacción activa con la audiencia.
